title: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
summary: Military historian Sarah Paine argues that global power dynamics for two millennia have been shaped by a divide between continental empires pursuing security through territorial conquest (China, Russia) and maritime powers pursuing prosperity through trade (Britain, US). The lecture frames Putin and Xi's actions through this geographical and historical lens.
writeup: Military historian Sarah Paine traces a two-millennia divide between continental empires that pursue security through territorial conquest (China, Russia) and maritime powers that pursue prosperity through trade (Britain, the United States). The lecture applies this framework to read the present actions of Putin and Xi through a geographical and historical lens. The central takeaway: a continental security doctrine favoring land control sits opposite a maritime logic favoring commerce, and that structural contrast continues to shape global power dynamics.
